### 1300_02400_HSE_Performance_Indicators.md

# HSE Performance Indicators

## Introduction

This document establishes a comprehensive framework of Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Performance Indicators for [Company Name]. The framework provides standardized metrics, measurement methods, and reporting procedures to effectively monitor, evaluate, and improve HSE performance across all organizational levels and operational areas.

## Scope of Work

### Purpose
To develop and implement a robust HSE performance measurement system that provides meaningful insights into organizational HSE performance, enables data-driven decision making, and supports continuous improvement initiatives.

### Responsibilities
- **HSE Director**: Overall performance indicator framework development and maintenance
- **Department Managers**: Implementation of performance measurement within their areas
- **HSE Specialists**: Data collection, analysis, and reporting support
- **All Employees**: Participation in performance measurement activities

### Timelines
- Framework development: Ongoing
- Data collection: Continuous real-time and periodic
- Reporting: Monthly, quarterly, and annually
- Review: Semi-annual framework evaluation
- Updates: As business needs evolve

### Deliverables
- Comprehensive HSE performance indicator framework
- Data collection and reporting procedures
- Performance dashboards and scorecards
- Trend analysis and benchmarking reports
- Continuous improvement recommendations

## Performance Indicator Framework

### Indicator Categories
- **Leading Indicators**: Proactive measures predicting future performance
- **Lagging Indicators**: Reactive measures reflecting past performance
- **Monitoring Indicators**: Real-time operational performance measures
- **Outcome Indicators**: Strategic goal achievement measures

### Measurement Principles
- Relevance to organizational HSE objectives
- Measurability and data availability
- Consistency and standardization
- Timeliness and frequency of measurement
- Actionability and improvement potential

### Data Quality Standards
- Accuracy and reliability of data sources
- Consistency in data collection methods
- Completeness of data coverage
- Timeliness of data reporting
- Validation and verification processes

## Leading Indicators

### Proactive Safety Activities
- **Safety Training Completion Rate**: Percentage of required safety training completed
  - Target: 100% completion within required timeframes
  - Measurement: Monthly tracking of training records
  - Responsible: HR and Department Managers

- **Safety Observations Conducted**: Number of safety observations per employee
  - Target: Minimum 2 observations per employee per quarter
  - Measurement: Observation reporting system tracking
  - Responsible: Supervisors and Safety Coordinators

- **Hazard Identification Reports**: Number of hazards reported by employees
  - Target: Continuous improvement in reporting rates
  - Measurement: Hazard reporting system tracking
  - Responsible: All Employees, HSE Coordinators

- **Safety Committee Participation**: Employee participation in safety committees
  - Target: 100% representation from all work areas
  - Measurement: Meeting attendance records
  - Responsible: Safety Committee Chairs

### Risk Management Activities
- **Risk Assessments Completed**: Percentage of required risk assessments completed
  - Target: 100% completion for all operations
  - Measurement: Risk assessment database tracking
  - Responsible: HSE Specialists, Department Managers

- **Control Measures Implementation**: Status of risk control measure implementation
  - Target: 100% implementation within agreed timeframes
  - Measurement: Action tracking system
  - Responsible: Responsible Managers

- **Contractor HSE Performance**: Contractor compliance with HSE requirements
  - Target: 95% compliance rate
  - Measurement: Contractor audit and monitoring results
  - Responsible: Procurement, Project Managers

### Training and Competency
- **Competency Assessments Completed**: Percentage of required competency assessments
  - Target: 100% completion within required intervals
  - Measurement: Competency management system
  - Responsible: HR, Department Managers

- **Training Effectiveness Scores**: Average scores from training evaluations
  - Target: Average score of 4.0 or higher (5-point scale)
  - Measurement: Training evaluation surveys
  - Responsible: Training Department

## Lagging Indicators

### Incident Statistics
- **Total Recordable Injury Rate (TRIR)**: Number of recordable injuries per 200,000 hours worked
  - Target: Industry benchmark or improvement trend
  - Measurement: Incident reporting system
  - Responsible: HSE Department

- **Lost Time Injury Rate (LTIR)**: Number of lost time injuries per 200,000 hours worked
  - Target: Continuous reduction toward zero
  - Measurement: Incident reporting system
  - Responsible: HSE Department

- **Fatality Rate**: Number of fatalities per million hours worked
  - Target: Zero fatalities
  - Measurement: Incident reporting system
  - Responsible: HSE Department

- **Near Miss Reporting Rate**: Number of near miss reports per 1,000 employees
  - Target: Increasing trend indicating improved reporting culture
  - Measurement: Near miss reporting system
  - Responsible: All Employees, HSE Coordinators

### Environmental Incidents
- **Environmental Spill Volume**: Total volume of environmental spills
  - Target: Reduction to zero or regulatory compliance limits
  - Measurement: Environmental incident reporting
  - Responsible: Environmental Specialists

- **Regulatory Non-Compliance Events**: Number of regulatory violations or notices
  - Target: Zero violations
  - Measurement: Compliance monitoring system
  - Responsible: HSE Compliance Team

- **Environmental Fines**: Total monetary value of environmental fines
  - Target: Zero fines
  - Measurement: Financial and compliance records
  - Responsible: HSE Compliance, Finance

### Health Statistics
- **Occupational Illness Rate**: Number of occupational illnesses per 1,000 employees
  - Target: Reduction toward zero
  - Measurement: Medical surveillance records
  - Responsible: Occupational Health, HSE

- **Workers' Compensation Claims**: Number and cost of workers' compensation claims
  - Target: Reduction in frequency and cost
  - Measurement: HR and insurance records
  - Responsible: HR, HSE, Finance

## Monitoring Indicators

### Real-Time Safety Metrics
- **Safety Behavior Compliance**: Percentage compliance with safety behaviors
  - Target: 95% or higher compliance rate
  - Measurement: Safety observation cards
  - Responsible: Supervisors

- **Equipment Inspection Status**: Percentage of equipment inspections completed on schedule
  - Target: 100% completion
  - Measurement: Maintenance management system
  - Responsible: Maintenance Supervisors

- **Permit-to-Work System Usage**: Percentage of high-risk activities using permits
  - Target: 100% compliance
  - Measurement: Permit tracking system
  - Responsible: Operations Supervisors

### Environmental Monitoring
- **Air Quality Measurements**: Compliance with air quality standards
  - Target: 100% compliance with regulatory limits
  - Measurement: Continuous monitoring systems
  - Responsible: Environmental Specialists

- **Water Quality Parameters**: Compliance with water discharge standards
  - Target: 100% compliance with regulatory limits
  - Measurement: Laboratory analysis results
  - Responsible: Environmental Specialists

- **Waste Management Compliance**: Proper waste handling and disposal rates
  - Target: 100% compliance with regulations
  - Measurement: Waste tracking records
  - Responsible: Environmental Coordinators

## Outcome Indicators

### Strategic Performance
- **HSE Strategic Objective Achievement**: Percentage of strategic objectives met
  - Target: 100% achievement of defined objectives
  - Measurement: Strategic plan tracking
  - Responsible: HSE Director, Senior Management

- **Stakeholder Satisfaction**: Satisfaction levels of key stakeholders
  - Target: 85% or higher satisfaction rate
  - Measurement: Stakeholder surveys
  - Responsible: Communications, HSE

- **Regulatory Audit Results**: Performance in regulatory inspections
  - Target: No critical findings, improvement in ratings
  - Measurement: Audit reports and regulatory feedback
  - Responsible: HSE Compliance Team

### Business Impact
- **HSE Return on Investment**: Financial benefits from HSE investments
  - Target: Positive ROI demonstrating value creation
  - Measurement: Cost-benefit analysis
  - Responsible: HSE Director, Finance

- **Insurance Premium Trends**: Trends in insurance costs and coverage
  - Target: Stable or decreasing premiums
  - Measurement: Insurance records and market analysis
  - Responsible: Finance, Risk Management

- **Business Continuity**: Impact of HSE incidents on business operations
  - Target: Minimize operational disruption
  - Measurement: Business impact assessments
  - Responsible: Operations, HSE

## Data Collection and Management

### Data Sources
- Incident reporting systems
- Training management systems
- Environmental monitoring systems
- Maintenance management systems
- Human resources systems
- Financial systems
- Regulatory reporting systems

### Collection Methods
- Automated system data extraction
- Manual data entry and validation
- Survey and feedback collection
- Audit and inspection reporting
- Third-party data sources

### Data Validation
- Regular data quality checks
- Cross-referencing with source documents
- Statistical analysis for anomalies
- Peer review and verification
- External audit validation

## Reporting and Analysis

### Reporting Frequency
- **Daily**: Critical real-time indicators
- **Weekly**: Key operational indicators
- **Monthly**: Comprehensive performance summary
- **Quarterly**: Trend analysis and departmental reports
- **Annually**: Strategic performance review

### Report Distribution
- **Executive Level**: Strategic performance dashboards
- **Management Level**: Departmental performance reports
- **Supervisor Level**: Operational performance summaries
- **Employee Level**: Team performance indicators

### Analysis Methods
- Trend analysis and pattern recognition
- Benchmarking against industry standards
- Statistical process control methods
- Root cause analysis of performance gaps
- Predictive analytics for future performance

## Performance Improvement

### Continuous Improvement Process
- Regular review of indicator effectiveness
- Identification of performance gaps
- Development of improvement initiatives
- Implementation and monitoring of actions
- Evaluation of improvement results

### Best Practice Sharing
- Internal benchmarking across sites
- Industry best practice research
- Knowledge sharing sessions
- Continuous learning programs
- Innovation and technology adoption

### Performance Recognition
- Achievement-based recognition programs
- Improvement-focused reward systems
- Team and individual acknowledgments
- Public communication of successes
- Lessons learned documentation

## Appendices

### Appendix A: Detailed Indicator Definitions and Calculation Methods
### Appendix B: Data Collection Forms and Templates
### Appendix C: Reporting Templates and Formats
### Appendix D: Benchmarking Data Sources
### Appendix E: Statistical Analysis Procedures
### Appendix F: Performance Dashboard Examples

### 1300_02400_HSE_Policy_Documents.md

# HSE Policy Documents

## Introduction

This document outlines the Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) policy framework for [Company Name], establishing our commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety, health, and environmental protection in all our operations. Our HSE policy serves as the foundation for all business decisions and operational activities.

## Scope of Work

### Purpose
To establish and communicate the company's formal HSE policy that guides all employees, contractors, and stakeholders in maintaining safe and environmentally responsible operations across all business units.

### Responsibilities
- **Senior Management**: Ultimate responsibility for HSE policy development, communication, and implementation
- **HSE Manager**: Oversight of policy implementation and compliance monitoring
- **Department Heads**: Implementation of policy within their respective departments
- **All Employees**: Adherence to policy requirements and active participation in HSE initiatives

### Timelines
- Policy review: Annual
- Policy updates: As required based on regulatory changes or business needs
- Communication training: Quarterly for new employees, annually for existing staff

### Deliverables
- Formal HSE policy document
- Policy acknowledgment forms
- Training materials and records
- Annual policy review reports

## HSE Policy Statement

[Company Name] is committed to providing a safe and healthy workplace for all employees, contractors, and visitors. We will comply with all applicable HSE laws, regulations, and standards, and we will strive to exceed these minimum requirements wherever possible.

Our policy includes:
- Prevention of work-related injuries and illnesses
- Protection of the environment from our operations
- Continuous improvement of our HSE performance
- Active involvement of all employees in HSE matters

## Objectives

1. Achieve zero fatalities and serious injuries
2. Reduce reportable incidents by 25% annually
3. Maintain full compliance with all applicable regulations
4. Continuously improve environmental performance
5. Foster a positive HSE culture throughout the organization

## Procedures

### Policy Communication
- Distribution to all employees upon hire
- Regular review and acknowledgment
- Display in prominent locations
- Inclusion in contractor agreements

### Policy Review
- Annual management review
- Update as required for regulatory changes
- Stakeholder feedback incorporation
- Effectiveness measurement through audits

## Roles and Responsibilities

### Senior Management
- Demonstrate visible leadership and commitment
- Allocate necessary resources for HSE
- Ensure policy integration into business processes
- Lead by example in all HSE activities

### HSE Department
- Develop and maintain HSE policies
- Monitor compliance and performance
- Provide training and guidance
- Investigate incidents and recommend improvements

### All Employees
- Follow established procedures
- Report hazards and incidents
- Participate in training programs
- Contribute to continuous improvement

## Compliance Requirements

- Adherence to local, state, and federal regulations
- Industry best practices and standards
- Company-specific procedures and guidelines
- International standards where applicable

## Review and Improvement

This policy will be reviewed annually or whenever significant changes occur in operations, regulations, or industry standards. The review process will include:
- Performance evaluation against objectives
- Stakeholder feedback analysis
- Regulatory compliance assessment
- Continuous improvement opportunities identification

## Appendices

### Appendix A: Policy Acknowledgment Form
### Appendix B: HSE Policy Distribution List
### Appendix C: Regulatory Compliance Matrix
### Appendix D: Annual Review Checklist


---

### 1300_02400_HSE_QUESTIONNAIRE_ENHANCEMENT_PLAN.md

# HSE Questionnaire Processing Enhancement - Complex Form Handling

## Problem Identified

The current questionnaire detection logic is too simplistic for complex HSE (Health, Safety, Environment) questionnaires. These documents have sophisticated structures that require advanced processing:

**HSE Questionnaire Complexity:**
- Multi-level hierarchical numbering (1, 1.1, 1.1a, etc.)
- Advanced scoring systems with weighted calculations
- Conditional logic and validation rules
- File upload capabilities for documentation
- Section-based organization with interdependencies
- JavaScript functionality for interactive forms
- Complex form validation and business rules

**Current Issue:**
- Simple questionnaire detection fails for complex HSE forms
- Server falls back to basic Excel processing
- Only 1 field generated instead of complex form structure
- User expectation not met for sophisticated HSE questionnaires

## Recommended Enhancement Strategy

### 1. Enhanced Document Type Detection
```javascript
// Enhanced detection for complex HSE forms
const detectComplexHSEForm = (rawData, filename) => {
  // HSE-specific patterns
  const hsePatterns = [
    /HSE/i.test(filename), // HSE in filename
    /questionnaire/i.test(filename),
    /safety/i.test(filename),
    /contractor/i.test(filename),
    /compliance/i.test(filename)
  ];
  
  // Complex structure indicators
  const complexPatterns = [
    /1\.\d+/.test(headerText), // Hierarchical numbering
    /score|rating/i.test(headerText), // Scoring systems
    /upload|file|document/i.test(headerText), // File uploads
    /weight|weighted/i.test(headerText), // Weighted calculations
    /section|part/i.test(headerText) // Section organization
  ];
  
  return {
    isHSE: hsePatterns.some(pattern => pattern),
    isComplex: complexPatterns.some(pattern => pattern),
    shouldUseAI: hsePatterns.some(pattern => pattern) || complexPatterns.some(pattern => pattern),
    processingMethod: hsePatterns.some(pattern => pattern) ? 'hse_ai_processing' : 'complex_ai_processing'
  };
};
```

### 2. AI-Powered Processing Pipeline
```javascript
// Route to AI processing for complex forms
const processComplexDocument = async (rawData, filename, hsePrompt) => {
  const analysis = detectComplexHSEForm(rawData, filename);
  
  if (analysis.shouldUseAI) {
    // Use AI processing with HSE prompt
    return await processExcelWithAI(rawData, filename, hsePrompt);
  }
  
  // Fall back to standard questionnaire processing
  return processQuestionnaireExcel(rawData, filename);
};
```

### 3. Enhanced AI Prompt Integration
```javascript
// Better prompt selection based on document analysis
const getOptimalPrompt = (filename, documentType, contentAnalysis) => {
  if (contentAnalysis.isHSE) {
    return 'questionnaire_form_conversion'; // HSSE prompt
  }
  
  if (contentAnalysis.isComplex) {
    return 'complex_form_conversion'; // Enhanced processing prompt
  }
  
  return 'standard_form_conversion'; // Basic processing prompt
};
```

## Implementation Steps

1. **Update Server Detection Logic**
   - Enhance `detectQuestionnaireFormat()` to handle HSE complexity
   - Add AI processing pipeline for complex forms
   - Improve prompt selection based on document analysis

2. **AI Service Integration**
   - Ensure DocumentStructureExtractionService handles HSE prompts
   - Add fallback for non-HSE complex forms
   - Implement proper error handling for AI processing

3. **Client-Side Enhancements**
   - Update form display for complex questionnaire structures
   - Handle field behaviors (editable/readonly/ai_generated) properly
   - Support for file upload and scoring systems

4. **Testing Framework**
   - Test with real HSE questionnaire files
   - Validate complex form generation
   - Ensure proper error handling for edge cases

## Expected Outcomes

- ✅ Complex HSE questionnaires processed correctly
- ✅ Full form structure with hierarchical fields
- ✅ AI-generated content for appropriate fields
- ✅ Proper field behavior classification
- ✅ Enhanced user experience for complex forms

## Next Steps

1. Implement enhanced detection logic in `server/api/process/form.js`
2. Test with real HSE questionnaire files
3. Validate AI processing pipeline
4. Update client-side form rendering
5. Create comprehensive test suite for complex forms

### 1300_02400_HSE_Responsibility_Matrix.md

# HSE Responsibility Matrix

## Introduction

This document establishes a comprehensive Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Responsibility Matrix that clearly defines roles, responsibilities, and accountabilities for HSE management across all levels of [Company Name]. The matrix ensures clarity of expectations and promotes effective coordination of HSE activities throughout the organization.

## Scope of Work

### Purpose
To provide a clear delineation of HSE responsibilities across all organizational levels, functions, and operational areas, ensuring comprehensive coverage of HSE management activities and accountability for performance outcomes.

### Responsibilities
- **HSE Director**: Development and maintenance of the responsibility matrix
- **Human Resources**: Integration of HSE responsibilities into job descriptions
- **Department Heads**: Implementation and communication of role-specific responsibilities
- **All Employees**: Understanding and execution of assigned HSE responsibilities

### Timelines
- Matrix development: Ongoing
- Annual review: Q1 each year
- Updates: As organizational changes occur
- Communication: Immediate upon changes
- Training: Initial upon hire, annual refresher

### Deliverables
- Comprehensive HSE responsibility matrix
- Role-specific HSE responsibility documents
- Integration into organizational charts
- Training and communication materials
- Performance accountability measures

## Responsibility Matrix Framework

### Organizational Structure
- Corporate leadership level
- Division/department level
- Site/facility level
- Supervisory level
- Individual contributor level

### HSE Functional Areas
- Policy development and communication
- Risk assessment and management
- Training and competency development
- Incident investigation and analysis
- Emergency response and preparedness
- Compliance management and auditing
- Performance monitoring and reporting
- Continuous improvement and innovation

### Responsibility Levels
- **Accountable (A)**: Ultimate responsibility and authority for outcomes
- **Responsible (R)**: Primary executor of activities and tasks
- **Consulted (C)**: Provides input and expertise
- **Informed (I)**: Kept aware of decisions and progress

## Corporate Leadership Responsibilities

### Chief Executive Officer
- **Policy**: Accountable for HSE policy statement and commitment
- **Resources**: Accountable for adequate HSE resource allocation
- **Performance**: Accountable for HSE performance outcomes
- **Culture**: Responsible for visible leadership in HSE matters

### Chief Operating Officer
- **Integration**: Responsible for HSE integration into business processes
- **Implementation**: Accountable for HSE program implementation
- **Review**: Responsible for regular HSE performance reviews
- **Improvement**: Accountable for continuous improvement initiatives

### Chief Financial Officer
- **Budget**: Accountable for HSE budget allocation and approval
- **Investment**: Responsible for HSE capital investment decisions
- **ROI**: Accountable for measuring HSE return on investment
- **Insurance**: Responsible for risk transfer and insurance programs

### General Counsel
- **Compliance**: Accountable for legal compliance with HSE regulations
- **Liability**: Responsible for HSE-related legal risk management
- **Litigation**: Accountable for HSE incident legal response
- **Contracts**: Responsible for HSE contract terms and conditions

## HSE Department Responsibilities

### HSE Director
- **Strategy**: Accountable for overall HSE strategy development
- **Programs**: Responsible for HSE program design and implementation
- **Compliance**: Accountable for regulatory compliance management
- **Performance**: Responsible for HSE performance monitoring and reporting

### HSE Manager
- **Operations**: Accountable for day-to-day HSE operations
- **Training**: Responsible for HSE training program delivery
- **Investigation**: Accountable for incident investigation management
- **Audit**: Responsible for internal audit program execution

### HSE Specialists
- **Technical**: Accountable for technical HSE expertise provision
- **Assessment**: Responsible for specialized risk assessments
- **Support**: Accountable for HSE technical support provision
- **Development**: Responsible for HSE procedure development

### HSE Coordinators
- **Coordination**: Accountable for HSE activity coordination
- **Documentation**: Responsible for HSE record maintenance
- **Communication**: Accountable for HSE information dissemination
- **Support**: Responsible for HSE administrative support

## Operational Department Responsibilities

### Operations Manager
- **Execution**: Accountable for HSE requirements execution
- **Supervision**: Responsible for supervisory HSE performance
- **Resources**: Accountable for operational HSE resource allocation
- **Compliance**: Responsible for operational compliance management

### Production Supervisors
- **Daily**: Accountable for daily HSE requirements implementation
- **Training**: Responsible for team HSE training delivery
- **Inspection**: Accountable for regular safety inspections
- **Reporting**: Responsible for HSE incident reporting

### Maintenance Manager
- **Maintenance**: Accountable for equipment maintenance safety
- **Contractors**: Responsible for contractor HSE management
- **Procedures**: Accountable for maintenance safe work practices
- **Training**: Responsible for maintenance team HSE training

### Project Manager
- **Project**: Accountable for project HSE performance
- **Planning**: Responsible for HSE integration into project planning
- **Contractors**: Accountable for project contractor HSE oversight
- **Reporting**: Responsible for project HSE performance reporting

## Support Function Responsibilities

### Human Resources Manager
- **Integration**: Accountable for HSE integration into HR processes
- **Recruitment**: Responsible for HSE competency assessment in hiring
- **Performance**: Accountable for HSE performance management
- **Training**: Responsible for general HSE awareness training

### Finance Manager
- **Tracking**: Accountable for HSE cost tracking and reporting
- **Budget**: Responsible for HSE budget monitoring
- **Analysis**: Accountable for HSE financial performance analysis
- **Investment**: Responsible for HSE return on investment calculation

### Procurement Manager
- **Supplier**: Accountable for supplier HSE performance standards
- **Equipment**: Responsible for safe equipment procurement
- **Contract**: Accountable for HSE contract terms development
- **Assessment**: Responsible for supplier HSE risk assessment

### IT Manager
- **Systems**: Accountable for HSE information system management
- **Security**: Responsible for HSE data security and privacy
- **Support**: Accountable for HSE technology support provision
- **Integration**: Responsible for HSE system integration

## Site/Facility Level Responsibilities

### Site Manager
- **Overall**: Accountable for overall site HSE performance
- **Resources**: Responsible for site HSE resource allocation
- **Compliance**: Accountable for site regulatory compliance
- **Emergency**: Responsible for site emergency response management

### Site HSE Coordinator
- **Coordination**: Accountable for site HSE activity coordination
- **Training**: Responsible for site HSE training delivery
- **Inspection**: Accountable for site safety inspections
- **Reporting**: Responsible for site HSE performance reporting

### Shift Supervisors
- **Shift**: Accountable for shift HSE performance
- **Communication**: Responsible for shift HSE communication
- **Incidents**: Accountable for shift incident response
- **Team**: Responsible for shift team HSE leadership

### Individual Contributors
- **Compliance**: Accountable for personal HSE compliance
- **Reporting**: Responsible for hazard and incident reporting
- **Participation**: Accountable for HSE activity participation
- **Improvement**: Responsible for HSE suggestion submission

## Cross-Functional Responsibilities

### Emergency Response Team
- **Preparedness**: Accountable for emergency preparedness
- **Response**: Responsible for emergency response execution
- **Equipment**: Accountable for emergency equipment maintenance
- **Training**: Responsible for emergency response training

### Incident Investigation Team
- **Investigation**: Accountable for thorough incident investigation
- **Analysis**: Responsible for root cause analysis
- **Reporting**: Accountable for investigation report completion
- **Follow-up**: Responsible for corrective action verification

### Training Committee
- **Curriculum**: Accountable for HSE training curriculum development
- **Delivery**: Responsible for training program delivery
- **Evaluation**: Accountable for training effectiveness evaluation
- **Improvement**: Responsible for training program improvement

### Audit Team
- **Planning**: Accountable for audit program planning
- **Execution**: Responsible for audit execution
- **Reporting**: Accountable for audit report preparation
- **Follow-up**: Responsible for audit finding resolution

## Performance Accountability

### Key Performance Indicators
- HSE responsibility fulfillment rates
- Incident rates by responsibility area
- Training completion and effectiveness
- Audit findings and corrective actions
- Stakeholder satisfaction with HSE performance

### Monitoring Mechanisms
- Regular responsibility performance reviews
- 360-degree feedback collection
- Peer assessment processes
- Management observation and feedback
- External stakeholder input

### Review and Improvement
- Annual responsibility matrix review
- Organizational change impact assessment
- Best practice identification and sharing
- Continuous improvement initiative implementation
- Performance gap analysis and addressing

## Appendices

### Appendix A: Detailed Role Responsibility Charts
### Appendix B: HSE Responsibility Communication Plan
### Appendix C: Performance Measurement Framework
### Appendix D: Training and Development Matrix
### Appendix E: Accountability and Consequences Framework
### Appendix F: Cross-Functional Coordination Procedures

### 1300_02400_HSSE_FORM_NAME_FIX_SUMMARY.md

# HSSE Form Name Fix - Complete Analysis & Resolution

## Issue Summary

**Problem**: HSSE Supplier/Contractor Evaluation Questionnaire forms were not receiving the anticipated form name from the client, resulting in generic "HSSE Supplier/Contractor Evaluation Questionnaire" titles instead of user-friendly names like "Form - HSEQS24001 Evaluation Response".

**Status**: ✅ **FIXED**

---

## Root Cause Analysis

### The Problem Flow

1. **Client Side** (`client/src/pages/01300-governance/components/01300-document-upload-modal.js` - Line 856):
   ```javascript
   formData.append('file', fileObj.file);
   formData.append('fileName', fileObj.name);
   formData.append('conversionMode', conversionMode);
   formData.append('companyId', 'EPCM');
   formData.append('organizationId', 'Organisation - EPCM');
   formData.append('projectId', 'Sample Project');
   formData.append('discipline', uploadDiscipline || '02400');
   formData.append('anticipatedFormName', anticipatedFormName || uploadFormName); // ✅ SENT BY CLIENT
   ```

2. **Server Side** (`server/src/routes/process-routes.js`):
   - **BEFORE FIX**: Server was NOT extracting `anticipatedFormName` from form fields
   - **ISSUE**: `enhancedMetadata.ui` object was created but missing the `anticipatedFormName` property
   - **RESULT**: Server fell back to generic title

### Metadata Flow Breakdown

```
CLIENT → FormData
  ├─ file: [File object]
  ├─ fileName: "HSEQS24001_Evaluation_Response.xlsx"
  ├─ anticipatedFormName: "Form - HSEQS24001 Evaluation Response" ✅ SENT
  └─ ...other fields

SERVER (BEFORE FIX) ↓
  ├─ Extract companyId ✅
  ├─ Extract organizationId ✅
  ├─ Extract projectId ✅
  ├─ Extract discipline ✅
  └─ Extract anticipatedFormName ❌ MISSING!

SERVER → enhancedMetadata.ui
  ├─ title: "HSSE Supplier/Contractor..." (GENERIC)
  └─ anticipatedFormName: undefined ❌ NOT SET

RESULT: Generic form title displayed
```

---

## The Fix

### Changes Made to `server/src/routes/process-routes.js`

#### 1. Extract `anticipatedFormName` from form fields (Line ~239):

```javascript
// Extract form data
companyId = Array.isArray(fields.companyId) ? fields.companyId[0] : fields.companyId;
organizationId = Array.isArray(fields.organizationId) ? fields.organizationId[0] : fields.organizationId;
projectId = Array.isArray(fields.projectId) ? fields.projectId[0] : fields.projectId;
discipline = Array.isArray(fields.discipline) ? fields.discipline[0] : fields.discipline;
department = Array.isArray(fields.department) ? fields.department[0] : fields.department;

// 🔧 FIX: Extract anticipatedFormName from form fields (sent by client)
const anticipatedFormName = Array.isArray(fields.anticipatedFormName) ? fields.anticipatedFormName[0] : fields.anticipatedFormName;
console.log('[FORM NAME FIX] Extracted anticipatedFormName from form fields:', anticipatedFormName);
```

#### 2. Add to `enhancedMetadata.ui` object (Line ~395):

```javascript
// Create enhanced unified metadata
const enhancedMetadata = {
  // ...existing fields...
  
  // 🔧 FIX: Add UI metadata with anticipatedFormName
  ui: {
    anticipatedFormName: anticipatedFormName || fileName.replace(/\.[^/.]+$/, ''),
    title: anticipatedFormName || unifiedMetadata.ui?.title || fileName.replace(/\.[^/.]+$/, ''),
    ...unifiedMetadata.ui
  },
  
  // Preserve original metadata
  ...unifiedMetadata
};

console.log('[FORM NAME FIX] Enhanced metadata with anticipatedFormName:', enhancedMetadata.ui?.anticipatedFormName);
```

### Fixed Metadata Flow

```
CLIENT → FormData
  ├─ anticipatedFormName: "Form - HSEQS24001 Evaluation Response" ✅

SERVER (AFTER FIX) ↓
  ├─ Extract anticipatedFormName ✅
  ├─ fields.anticipatedFormName = "Form - HSEQS24001 Evaluation Response"
  └─ Parse and store in variable

SERVER → enhancedMetadata.ui
  ├─ anticipatedFormName: "Form - HSEQS24001 Evaluation Response" ✅
  └─ title: "Form - HSEQS24001 Evaluation Response" ✅

EXCEL PROCESSING → formTitle
  ├─ Uses: enhancedMetadata.ui?.anticipatedFormName ✅
  └─ Result: "Form - HSEQS24001 Evaluation Response" ✅ CORRECT!

RESULT: User-friendly form title displayed ✅
```

---

## AI Field Behavior Issue

### Current Implementation

**Server-side** (`server/src/routes/process-routes.js` - Line ~885):
```javascript
const formFields = questionRows.map((row, index) => {
  // ...field extraction...
  
  const aiGeneratedAnswer = `[AI-ASSISTED RESPONSE PENDING] This question will be populated by AI analysis...`;
  
  return {
    id: fieldId,
    label: label,
    name: fieldId,
    type: "textarea",
    value: aiGeneratedAnswer,              // ✅ Pre-populated with AI placeholder
    ai_assisted: true,                     // ✅ Flag for AI processing
    ai_placeholder: "AI will analyze...",  // ✅ Placeholder text
    required: false                        // ✅ Editable, not required
  };
});
```

### User's Reported Issue

> "Fields are still editable and not AI generated"

### Analysis

The current implementation:
1. ✅ **Fields ARE pre-filled** with AI placeholder content
2. ✅ **Fields ARE marked** with `ai_assisted: true` flag
3. ✅ **Fields ARE editable** (intentionally - `required: false`)

### Expected vs Actual Behavior

| Aspect | Current Implementation | User Expectation? |
|--------|----------------------|-------------------|
| Pre-filled content | ✅ Yes - AI placeholder text | ✅ Matches |
| AI flag | ✅ `ai_assisted: true` | ✅ Matches |
| Editability | ✅ Editable (`required: false`) | ❓ Should fields be readonly? |
| AI icon/indicator | ❓ Not in server code | ❓ Missing visual indicator? |

### Clarification Needed

**Question for User**: What should "AI generated" mean for these fields?

**Option A - Current Behavior (Editable with AI Placeholder)**:
- Fields show AI placeholder text
- Users CAN edit the content
- AI will populate actual answers later
- Fields are marked with `ai_assisted: true`

**Option B - Readonly with AI Content**:
- Fields are readonly (`readonly: true`)
- AI generates actual content immediately
- Users CANNOT edit until AI processing completes

**Option C - Auto-populate then Editable**:
- AI generates actual answers first
- Fields are pre-filled with AI-generated content
- Users CAN still edit if needed

---

## Testing Recommendations

### 1. Form Name Test

**Steps**:
1. Upload HSSE questionnaire Excel file (e.g., `HSEQS24001_Evaluation_Response.xlsx`)
2. Enter custom form name: "Form - My Custom HSSE Evaluation"
3. Process the file
4. Check generated form title

**Expected Result**:
- Form title should be: "Form - My Custom HSSE Evaluation"
- NOT: "HSSE Supplier/Contractor Evaluation Questionnaire"

### 2. AI Field Configuration Test

**Steps**:
1. Process HSSE questionnaire
2. Check field configuration UI
3. Verify fields show correct behavior indicators:
   - 🔒 Read-Only
   - ✏️ Editable
   - 🤖 AI-Generated

**Expected Result**:
- Fields should have clear AI indicators
- Behavior should match user expectations
- Configuration should be saveable

### 3. Integration Test

**Steps**:
1. Upload → Process → Configure → Use Form
2. Verify complete workflow
3. Check database records

**Expected Result**:
- `governance_document_templates.template_name` = anticipated form name
- `form_metadata` contains correct anticipatedFormName
- All 82 fields extracted correctly

---

## Files Modified

1. ✅ `server/src/routes/process-routes.js` - Lines ~239, ~395
   - Added `anticipatedFormName` extraction
   - Added to `enhancedMetadata.ui` object

---

## Next Steps

1. ✅ **Deploy fix to server**
2. ⏳ **Test form name functionality**
3. ❓ **Clarify AI field behavior requirements**
4. ⏳ **Implement any needed AI field changes**
5. ⏳ **Update documentation**

---

## Additional Notes

### Form Name Generation Logic (Client-side)

The client generates anticipated form names from filenames:
```javascript
const generateAnticipatedFormName = (filename) => {
  let name = filename.replace(/\.[^/.]+$/, "");  // Remove extension
  name = name
    .replace(/_+/g, ' ')   // Underscores → spaces
    .replace(/-/g, ' ')    // Dashes → spaces
    .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')  // Collapse multiple spaces
    .trim();
  name = name.replace(/\b\w/g, l => l.toUpperCase());  // Capitalize words
  return `Form - ${name}`;
};
```

**Example**:
- Input: `HSEQS24001_Evaluation_Response.xlsx`
- Output: `Form - HSEQS24001 Evaluation Response`

### Database Storage

The anticipated form name is stored in:
- `governance_document_templates.template_name`
- `governance_document_templates.form_metadata.ui.anticipatedFormName`
- Used by Excel processing at Line ~927 in server code

---

## Commit Message Suggestion

```
fix(hsse): resolve anticipatedFormName not being sent to server

- Extract anticipatedFormName from form fields on server-side
- Add anticipatedFormName to enhancedMetadata.ui object
- Fix generic form titles for HSSE questionnaires
- Ensure user-friendly form names display correctly

Closes: HSSE form name issue
Refs: HSSE_FIXES_TODO.md
```

---

**Document Created**: 2025-10-20T05:50:00Z
**Status**: Fix Implemented, Testing Pending
**Priority**: High


---

### 1300_02400_HSSE_PROMPT_CONTENT.md

# 1300_02400 HSSE Prompt Content

## Overview

This document contains the specialized AI prompt framework for HSSE questionnaire template generation. The system has evolved from Excel spreadsheet processing to a dynamic template-based approach using pre-built HTML templates populated with configurable questionnaire data.

**Note**: Excel spreadsheet processing has been discontinued due to complexity. The system now uses a configuration-driven approach with pre-built HTML templates.

## Requirements

### Functional Requirements
- Generate dynamic HSSE questionnaire forms from pre-built HTML templates
- Populate templates with configurable questionnaire data (sections, questions, scoring)
- Preserve hierarchical numbering system (1, 1.1, 1.1a, etc.)
- Implement scoring system with weighted calculations
- Generate evaluation recommendations based on score thresholds
- Support multiple input types (text, textarea, number, file upload)

### Technical Requirements
- Use configuration-driven approach for questionnaire structure
- Store questionnaire configurations in discipline-specific database tables
- Dynamically populate HTML templates with configuration data
- Generate responsive, accessible HTML forms
- Include form validation and error handling
- Support questionnaire customization per discipline

## Implementation

### Prompt Metadata
- **ID:** 9430fe84-b564-4783-a214-177f78d690fb
- **Key:** questionnaire_form_conversion
- **Name:** HSSE Supplier Evaluation Questionnaire Form Conversion
- **Description:** Specialized prompt for converting Excel spreadsheet HSE evaluation templates into structured table-based HTML forms with scoring functionality
- **Is Active:** Yes
- **Created:** 2025-10-15T10:45:10.427895+00:00
- **Updated:** 2025-10-16T10:08:27.151626+00:00

### Current Implementation

# Dynamic Template-Based Questionnaire System

## Objective
Generate HSSE questionnaire forms using pre-built HTML templates populated with configurable questionnaire data. The system supports multiple questionnaire types through configuration rather than Excel processing.

## Architecture Overview
The system uses:
- **Pre-built HTML templates**: Complete, functional questionnaire templates (e.g., `1300_02400_HSSE_QUESTIONNAIRE_FORM.html`)
- **Configuration-driven data**: Questionnaire structure stored in database tables
- **Dynamic population**: HTML templates populated with configuration data at runtime
- **Discipline-specific customization**: Different questionnaire types per discipline

## Configuration Structure
Questionnaire configurations contain:
- Structured sections with hierarchical numbering (1, 1.1, 1.1a, etc.)
- Questions with different input types (text, textarea, radio, file upload)
- Scoring rules and weightings for evaluation
- Validation rules and requirements

## Target Requirements
Create an HTML form that:

### Structural Requirements
1. **Retain all original columns** from the spreadsheet:
   - Question Number (N°)
   - Question text
   - Additional language text (if applicable)
   - Document upload/input field
   - Answer input field
   - Score input (customizable scale)
   - Feedback textarea

2. **Organize by sections** matching the Excel structure:
   - Maintain the same section organization as source spreadsheet
   - Preserve section numbering and hierarchy

3. **Maintain hierarchical numbering** system from Excel

### Functional Requirements
1. **Scoring System**:
   - Each question should have a score input (customizable scale with decimal increments)
   - Section-level weighted scoring (configurable weights per section)
   - Global total score calculation
   - Automatic evaluation recommendation based on total score thresholds

2. **Weight Distribution**:
   - Configure section weights based on importance (percentages should total 100%)
   - Allow for manual override of section scores
   - Calculate weighted scores for each section

3. **Evaluation Recommendations**:
   - Define custom score thresholds for different recommendation levels
   - Generate automatic recommendations based on calculated scores
   - Support multiple recommendation categories (e.g., Approved, Conditional, Rejected)

### UI/UX Requirements
1. **Table-based layout** for questions with appropriate column distribution:
   - Maintain consistent column widths for readability
   - Ensure proper spacing and alignment
   - Support for various input types (text, select, file upload, number)

2. **Section headers** with visual distinction:
   - Clear section organization with visual hierarchy
   - Color-coded sections for easy navigation
   - Collapsible sections for better usability

3. **Interactive features**:
   - File upload functionality for document fields
   - Toggle to show/hide scoring system
   - Real-time score calculation and display
   - Form validation with user feedback

4. **Responsive design** that works on mobile devices:
   - Adaptable layout for different screen sizes
   - Touch-friendly controls and inputs
   - Print-friendly formatting options

## Technical Implementation Guidelines

### HTML Structure
```html
<!-- General Information Section -->
<div class="form-section">
    <h2>General Information</h2>
    <!-- Form fields for basic details, dates, evaluator -->
</div>

<!-- Each Evaluation Section -->
<div class="form-section">
    <h2>1. [Section Title]</h2>
    <div class="scoring-section">
        <!-- Scoring inputs for this section -->
    </div>
    <table class="evaluation-table">
        <thead>
            <tr>
                <th>[Column 1]</th><th>[Column 2]</th><th>[Column 3]</th><th>[Column 4]</th><th>[Column 5]</th><th>[Column 6]</th><th>[Column 7]</th>
            </tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
            <!-- Questions for this section -->
        </tbody>
    </table>
</div>
```

### JavaScript Functionality
1. **Score Calculation**:
   - Calculate weighted scores for each section based on configurable weights
   - Update global total score dynamically in real-time
   - Determine evaluation recommendation based on score thresholds

2. **Form Validation**:
   - Validate required fields with visual feedback
   - Ensure score inputs are within defined range
   - Handle conditional validation based on question types

3. **File Upload Handling**:
   - Support for common document formats (PDF, DOC, DOCX, JPG, JPEG, PNG)
   - File size validation and type checking
   - Document attachment management

4. **Interactive Features**:
   - Toggle visibility of scoring system
   - Collapsible sections for better navigation
   - Real-time calculation and display updates

## Configuration Schema

Questionnaire configurations are stored in discipline-specific database tables with the following structure:

### Questionnaire Configuration
```json
{
  "id": "unique_identifier",
  "name": "Questionnaire Name",
  "description": "Questionnaire description",
  "discipline": "safety|procurement|finance|etc",
  "sections": [
    {
      "id": "section_1",
      "title": "Section Title",
      "order": 1,
      "weight": 25,
      "questions": [
        {
          "id": "q1_1",
          "number": "1.1",
          "text": "Question text",
          "type": "radio|textarea|file|text",
          "required": true,
          "scoring": {
            "enabled": true,
            "scale": "1-5",
            "weight": 1
          },
          "options": ["Yes", "No"] // for radio/select
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "scoring_rules": {
    "total_max": 175,
    "recommendations": {
      "approved": { "min": 140, "label": "Approved - Excellent HSSE Management" },
      "conditional": { "min": 105, "max": 139, "label": "Conditional Approval - Requires Improvements" },
      "rejected_major": { "min": 70, "max": 104, "label": "Rejected - Major Improvements Needed" },
      "rejected_significant": { "max": 69, "label": "Rejected - Significant Compliance Issues" }
    }
  }
}
```

### Template Population Process
1. **Load Configuration**: Retrieve questionnaire config from database
2. **Select Template**: Choose appropriate HTML template based on questionnaire type
3. **Populate Sections**: Replace template placeholders with section data
4. **Generate Questions**: Create form inputs for each question
5. **Apply Scoring**: Configure scoring calculations and recommendations
6. **Validate Output**: Ensure generated HTML is complete and functional

## Special Considerations

1. **Multilingual Support**: Preserve additional language content while maintaining primary language
2. **Document Uploads**: Implement file upload fields where appropriate with proper validation
3. **Score Inputs**: Use configurable number inputs with appropriate min/max/step values
4. **Accessibility**: Ensure form is accessible with proper labels, ARIA attributes, and keyboard navigation
5. **Browser Compatibility**: Support modern browsers including mobile browsers with responsive design
6. **Form Validation**: Implement both client-side and server-side validation for data integrity
7. **Data Persistence**: Consider local storage or session management for form data
8. **Security**: Implement proper file upload security measures and input sanitization

## Expected Output

A complete HTML questionnaire that:
- Uses pre-built HTML templates as the foundation
- Dynamically populates sections and questions from database configurations
- Implements configurable scoring systems and evaluation recommendations
- Maintains professional, responsive design across all devices
- Supports file uploads, form validation, and real-time score calculations
- Can be customized per discipline while maintaining consistent functionality

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] Questionnaire configurations stored in database tables
- [ ] HTML templates dynamically populated with configuration data
- [ ] Scoring system working correctly with configurable rules
- [ ] Weighted scores calculated properly per section
- [ ] Evaluation recommendations display correctly
- [ ] File upload functionality working with validation
- [ ] Form validation implemented for all input types
- [ ] Responsive design tested across devices
- [ ] Print styles working for generated forms
- [ ] No JavaScript errors in template population
- [ ] All required fields marked appropriately
- [ ] Discipline-specific customizations working

## Status

- [x] **Architecture Updated**: Moved from Excel processing to configuration-driven templates
- [x] **Templates Available**: Pre-built HTML templates ready for dynamic population
- [x] **Database Schema**: Discipline-specific tables support questionnaire configurations
- [ ] **Implementation**: Dynamic template population system needs development
- [ ] **Testing**: End-to-end testing with configuration-driven questionnaires

## Version History

- **v2.0** (2025-12-11): Major architecture change - discontinued Excel processing, moved to configuration-driven templates
- **v1.2** (2025-11-11): Reformatted documentation to comply with 0000_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDE.md standards
- **v1.1** (2025-10-16): Updated prompt content for better Excel processing
- **v1.0** (2025-10-15): Initial HSSE questionnaire prompt creation

## Related Documentation

- [1300_02400_CONTRACTOR_VETTING_GUIDE.md](1300_02400_CONTRACTOR_VETTING_GUIDE.md) - Main contractor vetting system documentation
- [1300_01300_DOCUMENT_STRUCTURE_EXTRACTION_WORKFLOW.md](1300_01300_DOCUMENT_STRUCTURE_EXTRACTION_WORKFLOW.md) - Document processing workflow
- [docs/error-tracking/🎯 Format-Specific Processing Errors/1300_01300_EXCEL_PROCESSING_ERROR_TRACKING.md](docs/error-tracking/🎯 Format-Specific Processing Errors/1300_01300_EXCEL_PROCESSING_ERROR_TRACKING.md) - Excel processing error tracking

### 1300_02400_HSSE_SUPPLIER_EVALUATION_CONVERSION_PROMPT.md

# Generic Form Conversion Prompt for Table-Based Evaluation Forms

## Objective
Convert Excel spreadsheet content into a comprehensive HTML form using a standardized table-based layout with scoring functionality and evaluation recommendations. This template can be adapted for various evaluation forms while maintaining consistent formatting and functionality.

## Source Analysis
The Excel spreadsheet contains:
- Structured content with hierarchical numbering (1, 1.1, 1.1a, etc.)
- Multiple columns that should be preserved in the HTML conversion
- Organized sections covering various evaluation criteria
- Different question types including text areas, select dropdowns, and file uploads

## Target Requirements
Create an HTML form that:

### Structural Requirements
1. **Retain all original columns** from the spreadsheet:
   - Question Number (N°)
   - Question text
   - Additional language text (if applicable)
   - Document upload/input field
   - Answer input field
   - Score input (customizable scale)
   - Feedback textarea

2. **Organize by sections** matching the Excel structure:
   - Maintain the same section organization as source spreadsheet
   - Preserve section numbering and hierarchy

3. **Maintain hierarchical numbering** system from Excel

### Functional Requirements
1. **Scoring System**:
   - Each question should have a score input (customizable scale with decimal increments)
   - Section-level weighted scoring (configurable weights per section)
   - Global total score calculation
   - Automatic evaluation recommendation based on total score thresholds

2. **Weight Distribution**:
   - Configure section weights based on importance (percentages should total 100%)
   - Allow for manual override of section scores
   - Calculate weighted scores for each section

3. **Evaluation Recommendations**:
   - Define custom score thresholds for different recommendation levels
   - Generate automatic recommendations based on calculated scores
   - Support multiple recommendation categories (e.g., Approved, Conditional, Rejected)

### UI/UX Requirements
1. **Table-based layout** for questions with appropriate column distribution:
   - Maintain consistent column widths for readability
   - Ensure proper spacing and alignment
   - Support for various input types (text, select, file upload, number)

2. **Section headers** with visual distinction:
   - Clear section organization with visual hierarchy
   - Color-coded sections for easy navigation
   - Collapsible sections for better usability

3. **Interactive features**:
   - File upload functionality for document fields
   - Toggle to show/hide scoring system
   - Real-time score calculation and display
   - Form validation with user feedback

4. **Responsive design** that works on mobile devices:
   - Adaptable layout for different screen sizes
   - Touch-friendly controls and inputs
   - Print-friendly formatting options

## Technical Implementation Guidelines

### HTML Structure
```html
<!-- General Information Section -->
<div class="form-section">
    <h2>General Information</h2>
    <!-- Form fields for basic details, dates, evaluator -->
</div>

<!-- Each Evaluation Section -->
<div class="form-section">
    <h2>1. [Section Title]</h2>
    <div class="scoring-section">
        <!-- Scoring inputs for this section -->
    </div>
    <table class="evaluation-table">
        <thead>
            <tr>
                <th>[Column 1]</th><th>[Column 2]</th><th>[Column 3]</th><th>[Column 4]</th><th>[Column 5]</th><th>[Column 6]</th><th>[Column 7]</th>
            </tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
            <!-- Questions for this section -->
        </tbody>
    </table>
</div>
```

### JavaScript Functionality
1. **Score Calculation**:
   - Calculate weighted scores for each section based on configurable weights
   - Update global total score dynamically in real-time
   - Determine evaluation recommendation based on score thresholds

2. **Form Validation**:
   - Validate required fields with visual feedback
   - Ensure score inputs are within defined range
   - Handle conditional validation based on question types

3. **File Upload Handling**:
   - Support for common document formats (PDF, DOC, DOCX, JPG, JPEG, PNG)
   - File size validation and type checking
   - Document attachment management

4. **Interactive Features**:
   - Toggle visibility of scoring system
   - Collapsible sections for better navigation
   - Real-time calculation and display updates

## Data Mapping Instructions

For each row in the Excel spreadsheet:

1. **Section Headers**: Convert to `<h2>` elements with appropriate section numbering and titles
2. **Question Rows**: Create table rows with:
   - Question number in first column
   - Question text in second column
   - Additional language text in third column (if applicable)
   - Document upload field in fourth column
   - Answer input field in fifth column
   - Score input in sixth column
   - Feedback textarea in seventh column

3. **Hierarchical Structure**: Maintain the parent-child relationship based on numbering system (e.g., 1.1 as parent row, 1.1a, 1.1b, 1.1c as child rows)

4. **Input Types**: Map Excel cell types to appropriate HTML input elements:
   - Text fields → `<input type="text">` or `<textarea>`
   - Dropdown selections → `<select>` with options
   - Numeric scores → `<input type="number">` with appropriate min/max/step
   - File uploads → `<input type="file">` with multiple file support

## Special Considerations

1. **Multilingual Support**: Preserve additional language content while maintaining primary language
2. **Document Uploads**: Implement file upload fields where appropriate with proper validation
3. **Score Inputs**: Use configurable number inputs with appropriate min/max/step values
4. **Accessibility**: Ensure form is accessible with proper labels, ARIA attributes, and keyboard navigation
5. **Browser Compatibility**: Support modern browsers including mobile browsers with responsive design
6. **Form Validation**: Implement both client-side and server-side validation for data integrity
7. **Data Persistence**: Consider local storage or session management for form data
8. **Security**: Implement proper file upload security measures and input sanitization

## Expected Output

A complete HTML file that:
- Faithfully reproduces all content from the Excel spreadsheet
- Implements the scoring and vetting functionality from the reference HTML
- Is fully functional with JavaScript score calculation
- Has a professional, responsive design
- Can be printed or submitted electronically

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] All Excel columns preserved in HTML table
- [ ] Scoring system working correctly
- [ ] Weighted scores calculated properly
- [ ] Vetting recommendations display correctly
- [ ] File upload functionality working
- [ ] Form validation implemented
- [ ] Responsive design tested
- [ ] Print styles working
- [ ] No JavaScript errors
- [ ] All required fields marked appropriately

### 1300_02400_HSSE_corrected-prompt.md

# HSSE Questionnaire HTML Form Generator

## Primary Objective
Convert the provided HSSE Management and Culture questionnaire into a professional, fully-functional HTML form with scoring capabilities and document upload functionality.

## Input Data Structure
The questionnaire has:
- **7 main sections** with hierarchical numbering (1.1, 1.1a, 2.1, 2.1a, etc.)
- **Mixed question types**: Yes/No, descriptive answers, document requirements
- **Scoring requirements**: Each question needs evaluation scoring
- **Document uploads**: Policy documents, training matrices, organizational charts

## Required Output: Complete HTML Form

### HTML Structure Requirements
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>HSSE Management and Culture Questionnaire</title>
    <style>
        /* Professional styling for form sections, questions, scoring */
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <div class="questionnaire-container">
        <header>
            <h1>HSSE Management and Culture Questionnaire</h1>
            <div class="form-meta">
                <p>Company: <input type="text" id="company-name"></p>
                <p>Evaluation Date: <input type="date" id="eval-date"></p>
            </div>
        </header>

        <!-- Section 1: HSSE Management Involvement -->
        <section class="form-section" id="section-1">
            <h2>1. HSSE Management and Culture</h2>

            <div class="question-block">
                <h3>1.1 Involvement in HSE Aspects through Management</h3>
                <div class="sub-question">
                    <p><strong>1.1 a)</strong> What is the personal involvement of senior managers in HSE management?</p>
                    <textarea class="answer-field" placeholder="Describe senior management involvement..."></textarea>
                    <div class="scoring">
                        <label>Score (1-5): <input type="number" min="1" max="5" class="score-input"></label>
                        <textarea class="feedback-field" placeholder="Evaluation comments..."></textarea>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <!-- Additional sub-questions b) and c) -->
            </div>
        </section>

        <!-- Additional sections 2-7 with similar structure -->

        <div class="scoring-summary">
            <h2>Evaluation Summary</h2>
            <div id="total-score">Total Score: <span id="score-display">0</span>/175</div>
            <div id="recommendation">Recommendation: <span id="rec-display">Pending</span></div>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script>
        // JavaScript for score calculation, form validation, file uploads
    </script>
</body>
</html>
```

### Functional Requirements

#### 1. Question Organization
- **Main sections** as `<section>` elements with clear headings
- **Sub-questions** grouped under parent questions with indentation
- **Hierarchical numbering** preserved (1.1, 1.1a, 1.1b, etc.)

#### 2. Input Types by Question Type
- **Descriptive answers** → `<textarea>` fields
- **Yes/No questions** → Radio button groups
- **Document requirements** → `<input type="file">` with validation
- **Scores** → `<input type="number" min="1" max="5">`

#### 3. Scoring System
- **Individual scores**: 1-5 scale per question
- **Section totals**: Sum of question scores within each section
- **Overall total**: Sum of all section scores
- **Recommendations**: Automatic based on total score ranges

#### 4. File Upload Handling
- **Multiple formats**: PDF, DOC, DOCX, XLS, JPG, PNG
- **File validation**: Size limits, type checking
- **Upload feedback**: Success/error messages

### CSS Styling Requirements
- **Professional appearance**: Clean, corporate styling
- **Section organization**: Clear visual separation between sections
- **Responsive design**: Works on desktop and mobile
- **Print-friendly**: Optimized for PDF generation

### JavaScript Functionality
```javascript
// Core functions needed:
function calculateSectionScore(sectionId) {
    // Sum all scores in a section
}

function calculateTotalScore() {
    // Sum all section scores
}

function generateRecommendation(totalScore) {
    // Return recommendation based on score thresholds
}

function validateFileUpload(fileInput) {
    // Check file type and size
}

function updateScoreDisplay() {
    // Update UI with current scores
}
```

## Data Transformation Examples

### Example 1: Simple Descriptive Question
**Input:**
```
1.1 a) What is the personal involvement of senior managers in HSE management?
```

**Output:**
```html
<div class="sub-question">
    <p><strong>1.1 a)</strong> What is the personal involvement of senior managers in HSE management?</p>
    <textarea class="answer-field" placeholder="Describe senior management involvement..."></textarea>
    <div class="scoring">
        <label>Score (1-5): <input type="number" min="1" max="5" class="score-input" data-question="1.1a"></label>
        <textarea class="feedback-field" placeholder="Evaluation comments..."></textarea>
    </div>
</div>
```

### Example 2: Document Upload Question
**Input:**
```
2.1 a) Does your company have documentation relating to the HSE policy? If yes, attach a copy.
```

**Output:**
```html
<div class="sub-question">
    <p><strong>2.1 a)</strong> Does your company have documentation relating to the HSE policy? If yes, attach a copy.</p>
    <div class="answer-options">
        <label><input type="radio" name="q2-1a" value="yes"> Yes</label>
        <label><input type="radio" name="q2-1a" value="no"> No</label>
    </div>
    <div class="file-upload">
        <input type="file" accept=".pdf,.doc,.docx" multiple>
        <div class="upload-status"></div>
    </div>
    <div class="scoring">
        <label>Score (1-5): <input type="number" min="1" max="5" class="score-input" data-question="2.1a"></label>
        <textarea class="feedback-field" placeholder="Evaluation comments..."></textarea>
    </div>
</div>
```

## Scoring Logic
- **Perfect Score**: 35 questions × 5 points = 175 total points
- **Recommendation Thresholds**:
  - 140-175: "Approved - Excellent HSSE Management"
  - 105-139: "Conditional Approval - Requires Improvements"
  - 70-104: "Rejected - Major Improvements Needed"
  - Below 70: "Rejected - Significant Compliance Issues"

## Questionnaire Content to Convert

[Insert the full HSSE questionnaire content here]

## Final Output Requirements
- **Complete HTML file** ready to save and open in browser
- **All questionnaire content** converted to form fields
- **Functional scoring system** with automatic calculations
- **File upload capability** with validation
- **Professional styling** suitable for client presentations
- **Print-optimized** for PDF generation
- **Mobile responsive** design
